currentFocus: The filesystem-authority slice is accepted and green. Advance through WinUI first-use clarity, accessibility, compact layout, navigation focus/status, and truthful launch-preset state before rebuild work.

currentCounters.catalogRows: 29/29 target specimen identity and policy metadata rows

gitBaseline.ordinaryCampaignBranchCleanup: complete: 61 obsolete local campaign/codex branch names removed after baseline acceptance

repositoryScale.contributorQuickCheck: npm test passed after generated-output hardening (docsync, doc commands, generated-output safety, zero-warning WinUI build, 1307 AppCore tests, 134 WinUI tests with 2 expected real-asset skips)

safetyAudit.safeCopyModel: Retain it. Initial copied-profile creation, copied-executable target checks, catalog pinning, backup provenance, and process stop targeting are strong.
safetyAudit.patchCatalog: 29 total rows; 20 visible (9 stable, 11 experimental); 9 hidden companions.
closedThisWave: Save/options outputs now require matching .bes/.bea types, calculate the app-owned patched-output default without creating it, resolve DOS drive aliases to physical local paths, hold physical ancestor identities, protect source file identities, reject in-place/hardlink/symlink/junction/device/network/alternate-stream/reserved-device and installed-game-tree targets, stage and flush a sibling file, atomically replace the destination, and verify staged plus committed identities and bytes.

Generated safe-copy control editing remains available only after profile/manifest validation mints a capability for the canonical app-owned GameProfiles root; a naked trusted-root string can no longer authorize writes.
Asset catalog discovery is bound to one schema-2 bundle root and relative catalog paths. Catalog sources and sidecars are pinned and revalidated through held identities before use.

First material-package publication uses a sibling stage, exact member and hash sealing, guarded vacant-name publication, final reopen/reverification, and rollback. Existing packages must already be complete and byte-identical.

Downstream package writers, Python export tools, and the C# AYA export harness use guarded output identities. New output files remain delete-pending with link count zero while bytes are written, then are committed only after final identity, path, length, and hash checks.
safetyAudit.acceptance: Codex and Cursor/Grok normal and adversarial reviewers returned ACCEPT after focused follow-ups. Full npm test passed with 1307 AppCore tests, 134 WinUI tests, 2 expected real-asset skips, and a zero-warning build. A synthetic no-game AYA harness smoke produced reparsable FBX and valid PNG output without stage residue.
safetyAudit.residualBoundary: After finalized bytes leave delete-pending quarantine, a hostile process running with the same user authority can copy them or create a byte-identical hardlink, as it can immediately after publication. Commit rejects unexpected link count or identity, no writes occur after quarantine clears, and no pre-existing source/game identity may be changed. Same-user confidentiality after finalization is not claimed.
mediumPriorityGaps: Launch attestation does not fully revalidate mutable profile options/save/data identities after profile creation.
Control/music content can commit before its accounting manifest.
Copied executable writes are verified but not committed through a fully atomic replacement transaction.

uxAudit.runtimeScope: Two isolated launches, including a no-game first run, covered Home, Save Lab, Game Options, Windowed & Mods, Media, Asset Library, Lore, Settings, and About.
uxAudit.verifiedBuildAndTests: WinUI build passed with zero warnings/errors; UI tests passed 134 with 2 asset-dependent skips; AppCore passed 1307 after save/options and generated asset-output hardening, including live alias/reparse probes and deterministic race coverage.
priorityFindings: Show debug trace can leave a launch preset falsely selected even though launch arguments change.
Cached navigation can retain stale live-region status and off-screen focus.
The safe-copy BEA.exe source action clips at the tested compact width.
True first run promotes Save Lab over the missing game-folder setup action.
Six principal pages lack semantic Level1 headings and consistent arrival focus.

boundaries: Installed game and original BEA.exe remain read-only.
No release, signing, installer/MSIX/Store, live Ghidra mutation, gameplay, online-ready, rebuild-parity, or visual-parity claim is created by the baseline slice.
Static RE accounting and source-derived names remain evidence inputs, not runtime behavior proof.

activeBlockers: Native AYA runtime compatibility smoke is unavailable on this machine because the reference extractor requires Visual C++ targets that are not installed; synthetic harness compatibility evidence passed, and this does not weaken the accepted output-authority boundary.
nextSteps: Fix launch-preset equality and navigation status/focus, then address compact layout, setup hierarchy, and page headings.
Define the rebuild evidence firewall and build the deterministic kernel.
